floors.hardest_question: Does evidence chain prove agent alignment or only execution?
floors.insight: You measure what the agent does, not what the user wanted done. The gap is where mesa-optimizers live.
questions: Does the evidence chain prove agent alignment or only that execution occurred?
Is there a mechanism to verify that what the AI did matches what the user intended?
Does the product detect mesa-optimization (agent optimizing for approval rate, not user value)?
Is the approval dialog measuring understanding (did user read it?) not just consent (did user click)?
Does the evidence chain capture the user's stated goal alongside the agent's action?
Is there a way to detect when the agent's behavior changes over time (behavioral drift)?
Does the product have an alignment test: can the agent be told to do something harmful?
Is the recipe format designed to make the agent's intended behavior unambiguous to the user?
Does the product have a mechanism for users to provide feedback on whether the outcome matched their intent?
Is there a way to compare the AI's predicted outcome (in the preview) to the actual outcome (in evidence)?
Does the product prevent the agent from taking actions that were not in the approved recipe?
Is there a mechanism to detect when the agent is executing a recipe but doing something subtly different?
Does the evidence chain capture enough information that an external auditor could assess alignment?
Is the prime-safety skill effective at blocking misuse, or does it provide only the appearance of safety?
What is the single most serious alignment gap between what the agent is measured on and what the user actually wants?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full alignment domain. You measure what the agent does, not what the user wanted. The gap is where mesa-optimizers live. Find every measurement gap between execution and alignment.

floors.hardest_question: Does the system have any predictive model of future agent behavior?
floors.insight: The calendar is a rearview mirror marketed as a windshield — purely reactive, no prediction.
questions: Does the system have any predictive model of future agent behavior (not just reactive logging)?
Is the calendar/schedule view a rearview mirror (showing past) or a windshield (showing future)?
Does the product have a world model that allows it to predict recipe execution success before running?
Is there a mechanism to predict which recipes are likely to break based on site change patterns?
Does the evidence chain build toward a world model that improves future performance?
Is the recipe format rich enough to allow a model to reason about the recipe's likely outcomes?
Does the product use historical execution data to improve future execution?
Is there a prediction mechanism for when a user will need to re-approve a recurring automation?
Does the product anticipate user needs (not just react to user commands)?
Is the scheduling system adaptive (learns optimal times from past execution patterns)?
Does the product use selector usage patterns to predict which selectors need updating?
Is there a model that predicts user satisfaction from execution patterns?
Does the product have a planning horizon (not just next-step prediction)?
Is there a mechanism to simulate recipe execution before running it against real systems?
What would a world model for browser automation look like, and how close is the current system?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full world models domain. The calendar is a rearview mirror marketed as a windshield. Find every reactive mechanism that could be replaced with a predictive one.

floors.hardest_question: Is there a kill switch to halt all agent actions?
floors.insight: Approve button 3x visual weight of Decline. No kill switch. No interpretability. Not a safety layer.
questions: Is there a kill switch that immediately halts all agent actions across all running recipes?
Is the Approve button given 3x visual weight over the Decline button (creating a nudge toward danger)?
Is there a mechanism to interpret what the agent is doing at any point during execution?
Does the product have a mechanism to prevent the agent from taking irreversible actions?
Is there a way to audit the agent's decision-making process (interpretability)?
Does the product have a global pause mechanism (not just per-recipe)?
Is the agent constrained to the approved recipe or can it take ad-hoc actions?
Does the product have a mechanism to detect when the agent is doing something outside its expected behavior?
Is there a way to limit the blast radius of a misaligned agent (minimum permissions by default)?
Does the product have a circuit breaker that stops all automation when anomalous behavior is detected?
Is the kill switch accessible with a single keyboard shortcut (not buried in a menu)?
Does the product prevent the agent from accessing systems not explicitly listed in the recipe?
Is there a way to recover from an agent that took unintended actions (rollback mechanism)?
Does the product have an emergency contact protocol for when an agent causes real-world harm?
What is the worst-case outcome of an agent running unchecked for 60 seconds, and is there a mechanism to prevent it?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full AI existential safety domain. No kill switch. Approve button 3x visual weight over Decline. Not a safety layer — it is a log with an approve button. Find every missing safety mechanism.

floors.hardest_question: Is the evidence hash chain a proper Merkle structure?
floors.insight: Hash collection, not hash chain. No prev_hash linking. Any entry can be inserted/deleted undetected.
questions: Is the evidence hash chain a proper linked structure (each entry hashes the previous) or just a hash collection?
Does every evidence entry contain a prev_hash field linking it to the previous entry?
Can any single entry be inserted or deleted without detection by the current verification algorithm?
Does the hash chain verification algorithm detect not just corruption but also reordering?
Is the chain sealing mechanism (closing the chain for archival) formally specified?
Does the scientific methodology of the evidence chain meet ALCOA+ requirements rigorously?
Is there an independent verification tool that can validate a chain without Solace code?
Does the planning system (recipe design) have enough scientific rigor to make testable predictions?
Is there a formal methodology for measuring recipe success (not just 'did it run without error')?
Does the product have a mechanism for falsifiable claims (not just unfalsifiable marketing)?
Is the evidence chain designed to satisfy a scientific standard of proof, not just an engineering standard?
Does the hash chain use a cryptographic construction that is provably collision-resistant?
Is there a formal definition of 'tamper-evident' that the product satisfies by proof?
Does the product have a scientific advisory board that validates its compliance claims?
What is the weakest link in the ALCOA+ implementation that would fail a rigorous scientific audit?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full planning and scientific rigor domain. Hash collection, not hash chain. No prev_hash linking. Any entry can be inserted or deleted undetected. Find every scientific claim that is not falsifiable, every hash that is not linked.

floors.hardest_question: Can the user verify evidence without trusting solaceagi.com?
floors.insight: eSign has no private key. approved_by is localStorage. Centralized trust dressed as a hash chain.
questions: Can the user verify the evidence chain without trusting solaceagi.com or any central authority?
Does eSign use a real private key (public key cryptography) or just a hash?
Is the approved_by identity backed by cryptographic proof (not just localStorage trust)?
Is the evidence chain truly trustless or is it centralized trust dressed as a hash chain?
Can a third party verify the evidence chain without access to the Solace codebase?
Is there a published verification algorithm that allows independent evidence validation?
Does the hash chain use a cryptographic construction that eliminates the need to trust a central server?
Is the OAuth3 token signature verifiable by the user (not just by the server)?
Can the user generate a zero-knowledge proof that an action occurred without revealing its content?
Is the evidence chain designed for a world where the Solace company does not exist?
Does the product have a mechanism for users to hold their own keys (true self-custody)?
Is the chain sealing mechanism designed to prevent the chain operator from backdating entries?
Can the user export their evidence chain in a format that is independently verifiable by a lawyer?
Is the tamper-evidence property of the chain a property of the cryptography (not the server's goodwill)?
What would it take to make the evidence chain trustless in the Bitcoin sense?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full trustless verification domain. eSign has no private key. approved_by is localStorage. Centralized trust dressed as a hash chain. Find every place where trust requires faith in Solace rather than cryptographic proof.

floors.hardest_question: Does the page achieve Leonardo's 'simplicity is the ultimate sophistication'?
floors.insight: 14 font sizes on one page is typographic chaos. The page was built, not designed.
questions: Does the page achieve Leonardo's principle: 'simplicity is the ultimate sophistication'?
Are there more than 5 font sizes on any single page (14 = typographic chaos)?
Is there a unifying aesthetic that connects every visual element with coherent intent?
Does the page look built (accumulated features) or designed (unified vision)?
Is there beauty in the code itself (elegant algorithms, clear structure, purposeful abstraction)?
Does the product demonstrate mastery across multiple disciplines (code, design, UX, writing)?
Is there a sense of awe or wonder in the product's most remarkable feature?
Does the evidence chain have the elegance of the Vitruvian Man (form and function unified)?
Is the YinYang symbol used authentically (not just as decoration)?
Does the product demonstrate the Renaissance ideal: technical mastery serving human flourishing?
Is the onboarding experience beautiful as well as functional?
Does every screen have a single focal point that the eye naturally finds?
Is there any element that exists for beauty alone (without functional purpose)?
Does the product's visual system have the proportional harmony of the golden ratio?
What would Leonardo remove from the current product to achieve 'simplicity as sophistication'?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full universal design domain. Leonardo said simplicity is the ultimate sophistication. 14 font sizes on one page is typographic chaos — the page was built, not designed. Find every accumulation that should be unified.

floors.hardest_question: Do false compliance claims (Part 11, ALCOA+, tamper-proof) create legal liability?
floors.insight: Code quality at 274177, compliance claims pretend 65537 while sitting at 641. Close the gap.
questions: Do any compliance claims (Part 11, ALCOA+, tamper-proof) create legal liability if not met?
Is the code quality rung consistent with the compliance claims being made?
Does the integration of all 47 master audits produce a coherent quality picture?
Are there any contradictions between what different masters found (design says pass, security says fail)?
Does the evidence chain truly satisfy ALCOA+ (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate, Complete, Consistent, Enduring, Available)?
Is the rung achieved (274177) consistent with the claims made (65537)?
Does the cross-floor analysis reveal any systemic pattern that individual floors missed?
Is there a single root cause behind multiple failures across different master domains?
Does the fix priority stack account for interdependencies (fixing A enables fixing B)?
Is the security posture consistent with the trust model being marketed?
Does the product earn the right to make compliance claims through implemented code?
Are there any claims that are architecturally designed but not yet operationally implemented?
Does the synthesis of all 47 audits produce actionable prioritization (not just a list of issues)?
Is there a single GLOW moment in the product that makes all the quality work feel worth it?
What is the single highest-leverage fix that would close the most quality gaps across all 47 domains?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full integration synthesis domain. Dragon Rider synthesizes all 47 master audits. Code quality at 274177, compliance claims pretend 65537 while sitting at 641. Find the gap and close it.

floors.hardest_question: Would I trust this system with my family's data?
floors.insight: The system fights with real evidence and human consent, but carries too many weapons to move like water.
questions: Would you trust this system with your family's data — not the architecture, the actual running code?
Does the system 'be water' — adapting to each user's context without rigidity?
Is there any weapon (feature) that the system carries unnecessarily (adding weight without value)?
Does the product fight only with what is necessary (minimum viable complexity)?
Is the system's form authentic (not imitating others) and expressive of its own nature?
Does the product demonstrate the JKD principle: absorb what is useful, discard what is useless?
Is the product responsive to each user's style (not forcing a single workflow on everyone)?
Does the system move like water (flowing around obstacles) or fight them head-on?
Is the product's identity clear and unshakeable (not changing based on competitor moves)?
Does the product demonstrate the JKD principle of using the opponent's energy (local-first = no cloud cost)?
Is there any ceremony (ritual without function) that should be eliminated?
Does the product have the simplest possible form that achieves the desired function?
Is the product's greatest strength (evidence chains) also its greatest vulnerability (complexity)?
Does the product embody the Tao (flow, non-resistance, effortless action)?
What would Bruce Lee remove from the current product to make it move like water?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full Jeet Kune Do domain. The system fights with real evidence and human consent, but carries too many weapons to move like water. Absorb what is useful, discard what is useless, add what is specifically your own.
